items.title: Chisholm exits after collision with Dominguez
items.summary: Yankees second baseman Jazz Chisholm Jr. left Monday night's 7-3 loss to the Tigers following a violent collision with right fielder Jasson Domínguez and was placed in concussion protocol.

items.title: Yankees' Fried to face hitters, including Stanton
items.summary: Max Fried is slated to face hitters for the first time since going on the injured list in mid-May in a live batting practice session, manager Aaron Boone said, and the plan is for Giancarlo Stanton, also on the IL, to face him.

items.title: Yankees Rivalry Roundup: Blue Jays snap six-game losing streak in Bichette’s return to Toronto
items.summary: The Yankees offense keeps finding new lows to achieve. They’ve managed one or fewer hits through seven innings in back-to-back games, have lost five straight, and mustered just 12 hits across their last four games, which is the fewest hits over a four-game span in franchise history. Yes, three of their best hitters are out […]

items.title: Yankees news: Jazz Chisholm Jr. leaves Monday’s game with injury
items.summary: NBC Sports: In case you missed it, Jazz Chisholm Jr. exited Monday’s game against the Tigers early. While tracking a fly ball, he collided with Jasson Domínguez, taking the outfielder’s arm straight to the head.
